channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know on the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic collapsed nearly an hour after a merengue concert began, killing at least 98 people and injuring at least 160.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The roof collapses at a Dominican Republic nightclub, killing at least 98 people
item.description: Rescuers are frantically searching overnight for more survivors in the rubble of the iconic Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: King Charles III’s fairy tale isn’t the one we remember as marriage to Camilla reaches 20-year mark
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met more than 50 years ago but their romance had to survive meddling families, marriages to other people and no small amount of public ridicule before they finally married.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Asia and European shares sink as US tariffs take effect
item.description: Asian and European shares sank again on Wednesday as the latest set of U.S. tariffs, including a 104% levy on Chinese imports, went into effect.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social worker imprisoned nearly 4 years for mediation attempts at Hong Kong protests
item.description: A Hong Kong social worker has been sentenced to three years and nine months in prison for rioting after she tried to mediate between protesters and police at the 2019 anti-government protests.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US defense secretary says China is a threat to Panama Canal, spurring a fiery response from Beijing
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday the Panama Canal faces ongoing threats from China but that together the United States and Panama will keep it secure.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korean leader's sister denounces denuclearization calls, saying nuclear status is permanent
item.description: The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un on Wednesday mocked Washington and its Asian allies for what she called their “daydream” of denuclearizing the North, insisting that the country will never give up its nuclear weapons program.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected US strikes in Yemen kill at least 6 people, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded the area around Yemen's Red Sea port city of Hodeida on Tuesday night, killing at least six people, the Iran-backed Houthi rebels said. The strikes hit around Hodeida's al-Hawak district, the rebels said, and wounded 16 people.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The IMF reaches a deal with troubled Argentina on a $20 billion bailout
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has reached a preliminary agreement with Argentina on a $20 billion bailout package, providing a reprieve to President Javier Milei as he seeks to overturn the country’s old economic order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congo repatriates 3 Americans who had faced death sentences over a failed coup plot
item.description: LAGOS, Nigeria (AP) — The three Americans convicted of participating in a botched coup attempt in Congo last year have been repatriated to the United States, days after their death sentences were commuted to life imprisonment, Congo's presidency said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Slain Hamas hostage's family fights for the release of those still in Gaza
item.description: Oded Lifshitz, an 83-year-old great-grandfather, returned to Israel in a coffin more than 500 days after he was taken from his home. Now, his family is fighting for the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Americans say Trump is favoring Russia too much in new poll
item.description: More Americans view President Trump as favoring Russia too much in negotiations to end the war with Ukraine, according to a survey conducted by the Pew Research Center and published on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to host El Salvador's president amid US deportation efforts
item.description: President Trump will welcome the leader of El Salvador at the White House next week to tout cooperation between the two nations as the U.S. sends deported migrants they claim are gang members to a massive El Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Bessent: Tariff negotiations result of 'massive inflow' of calls, not markets
item.description: Treasury Sec. Scott Bessent said on Tuesday that President Trump’s apparent willingness to engage in tariff negotiations is a result of a “massive inflow” of calls from countries, not the downturn in global markets.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: State Department reviewing 'incident' with Rubio's security in Brussels
item.description: The State Department said it is reviewing an “incident” with Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s security detail during last week’s trip to Europe. A Diplomatic Security Service (DSS) shift supervisor reportedly was arrested and released following an altercation with local police.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Top Democrat announces measure to curb Trump on tariffs
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden (Ore.), the top Democrat on the Senate Finance Committee, announced Tuesday he’s introducing a resolution to thwart President Trump’s latest tariffs actions.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, The Hill

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea will hold a presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon's successor
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea will hold a snap presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon Suk Yeol's successor after the conservative was ousted over his imposition of martial law late last year.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: It’s springtime in Germany and people are obsessing about white asparagus
item.description: BEELITZ, Germany (AP) — Asparagus season has arrived in Germany, signaling the unofficial beginning of springtime. There is no other vegetable that Germans obsess about as much as white asparagus.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Contract for Hong Kong company to operate Panama Canal ports had irregularities, audit finds
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama’s comptroller authority said Monday an audit found irregularities in the renewal of a 25-year port concession in the interoceanic canal and it would request an investigation into the authorization of the contract to a Hong Kong company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistani security forces kill 9 militants in a raid near the Afghan border, the military says
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces raided a militant hideout in the restive northwest near the Afghan border, killing nine militants, the military said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu to meet at White House as Israel seeks tariff relief, discussions on Iran, Gaza hostages
item.description: President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu set for White House meeting to discuss ending U.S. tariffs on Israel, the war in Gaza, the release of hostages and Iran's nuclear threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea presidential ouster part of Chinese strategy to 'expand its regional influence,' expert says
item.description: One analyst, as well as supporters of South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, says China backed Yoon's ouster in South Korea to weaken U.S. ties and expand its influence by propping up Beijing-friendly political allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
